about.p1: Our mission is to offer the market a line of catamaran boats that stand out for their navigation efficiency. We strive to make the production of each vessel unique and not only meet but exceed the client's expectations.
about.p2: Our modern construction approach, combined with top-tier suppliers and constant training of our professionals, gives us the confidence that we are producing high-quality boats, ensuring they maintain their attributes for many years.
about.p3: We will continue to develop catamarans that are a reference in the market, serving the most demanding consumers, those who expect an incomparable experience that takes them beyond simply navigating.

clients.quote: "Looking at the bow of this catamaran, the sensation was that we were flying over the waves, supported by a cushion of air. The urge was to accelerate more, hypnotized as we were by the gentle pitch of the 8XF, totally stable in its natural element (read rough sea)..."
clients.author: (Marcio Dottori, Nautical Magazine)

quality.title: Quality
quality.text: Our commitment to quality leads us to use the most advanced materials and construction methods, resulting in models that ensure superior and memorable navigation. Awards, magazine articles, and customer feedback motivate us to keep our products unique.

efficiency.title: Efficiency
efficiency.text: The catamarans we produce undergo the vacuum infusion process, ensuring properties superior to hand-laminated hulls and eliminating the use of wood in our production. Being lighter and highly resistant, navigating with this type of hull ensures engine and fuel economy even at high speeds.

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.text: Our development team is constantly testing new technologies to improve our catamarans, always seeking to exceed customer expectations and offer the newest in the segment.

history.title: Catamaran History
history.p1: How the catamaran emerged: Catamarans emerged in Micronesia and Polynesia out of a need to improve stability and speed, as an alternative to monohulls. It was through these vessels that these people occupied the Pacific islands (including the colonization of Hawaii). The first Westerners to come into contact with these vessels were the Spanish, who were very impressed with the speed of this type of boat.
history.p2: How it developed: In the 1950s in England, the first modern sailing catamarans for competition were developed. The first generation of designers and builders of modern multihulls emerged with a growing variety of sailing and motor vessels for sport and leisure. Since then, with the development of materials and construction techniques, catamarans have become an option for various areas of naval engineering, with their most varied forms, whether conventional, symmetrical, or asymmetrical hulls.
history.p3: The catamaran today: With the development of materials and structural knowledge, catamarans are gaining more space in navigation every day, whether for passenger or cargo transport, as in the case of ferry boats, or for leisure, such as sailboats, racing boats, and cruisers. In the transport area, the catamaran has proven to be an excellent solution for short lines of up to 1000 km (540 miles), with speeds exceeding 30 knots, becoming an effective competitor to the already saturated means of transport in large production centers. The catamaran will always be an option whenever economy, comfort, and stability are project requirements.

infusion.title: Construction Process: Vacuum Infusion
infusion.p1: What it is: The vacuum infusion process is one of the most innovative processes in the nautical industry. It is a method that often ends up being more efficient and practical than manual lamination, for example. Each case is different; boats require specific construction processes and methods depending on their size and use.
infusion.p2: Where it applies: The infusion method is the process used for the construction of high-performance and quality boats, seen in the manufacture of boats by large shipyards around the world. It is an industrial-scale method, and thus requires structure and experience to be performed.
infusion.p3: How it works: In the vacuum infusion process, all material, PVC foams, and fabrics are placed dry in the ready mold, and thus wrapped in a plastic vacuum bag. When the vacuum pump is activated, the air will leave the plastic and the resin will be transferred, through hoses connected to a resin bucket, into the fiber layers by the differential action of internal and external pressure.
infusion.p4: Mechanical characteristics: Laminates manufactured by the infusion process are stronger than conventional manually manufactured laminates, as their impregnation between the fibers reaches 60%, while manual lamination reaches only 20 to 30% fiber content. With the infusion method, we leave aside environmental and unhealthiness problems, as there is no waste of material and there is no direct contact between the builder and the resins.
infusion.p5: Advantages of Infusion: Boat constructions using vacuum infusion invariably use the sandwich system, which means they will present greater thermal insulation, making it economical when thinking about the use of air conditioning on the boat and the energy produced by the generator system to power them. Another economy to be observed is that of resin and fibers. Since the resin is not touched during lamination, only during infusion into the laminate, waste is avoided. In the case of fibers, the fact is that during manual lamination many fibers end up falling on the floor, or are partially impregnated, without being able to be reused; in the infusion method, since lamination is done dry, the fiber can be pre-cut and adjusted in place with precision.
infusion.p6: Considerations: High-performance catamaran boats require a construction process that ensures high strength and low weight. Among the construction processes employed in the nautical industry, the vacuum infusion process has shown to be the best option to ensure the highest quality standards.

hulls.title: Types of Catamaran Hulls
hulls.intro: Currently, there is an infinity of catamaran hull shapes. Project requirements such as speed, expected behavior, motorization, and costs will define which type of hull is most suitable. Catamaran boats generally fall into three main types listed below:
hulls.semi: Semi-displacement hulls: This type of hull has a rounder bottom design, generating a profile more suitable for absorbing impact with waves. These types of hulls present a well-defined optimal speed range, but are less efficient for high speeds.
hulls.sym: Symmetrical planing hulls: Planing catamarans are those that feature predominantly flat hull sections. Geometry that promotes greater lift and facilitates the planing regime. Being more indicated for cases where speed and low fuel consumption are project requirements.
hulls.asym: Asymmetrical planing hulls: This type of hull is dedicated to boats that will navigate at high speeds. Although it generally requires more powerful motorization, this type of hull gains efficiency at high speeds.

fishing.title: Fishing Boat, How to Choose...
fishing.intro: In addition to appropriate techniques and equipment, the open sea fisherman needs a boat capable of taking him in the best, fastest, and most comfortable way to his favorite fishing spot. For this, it is necessary to use boats that meet minimum requirements of economy, seaworthiness, safety, and autonomy. The correct choice guarantees much more pleasure for the fisherman and increases the chances of fish on the line. Mastro D'Ascia develops catamaran boats approved for open sea navigation that offer the maximum in economy and seaworthiness.
fishing.bottom: Bottom fishing: As the name implies, this type of fishing is carried out in deep places. It consists of placing the bait, preferably natural ones, at the bottom. The boat used needs to have circulation space and work with the fish. Indicated model: NOMAD 7CC, TITAN CC.
fishing.offshore: Offshore or ocean fishing: For this modality, boats with powerful engines are indicated to cover long distances in long-duration fishing trips. Indicated models: NOMAD 7XF, NOMAD 8XF, and TITAN CC.
fishing.spear: Spearfishing: In this modality, the fisherman uses special guns to shoot fish underwater. Generally, long distances are covered and a lot of space is needed to store all equipment. Indicated models: NOMAD 7CC and NOMAD 7XF.

maintenance.title: Maintenance Routine
maintenance.intro: Your vessel needs some care to always be like new. Establish a verification routine for some fundamental items. Below we suggest a standard:

weekly.l2: Remove the protective cover to circulate air and take sun.
weekly.l3: When returning from the sea, flush the outboard motor (flushing is running with fresh water to remove salt residue and prevent its accumulation inside the motor).
weekly.l4: Never store the vessel wet and cover with the canvas. Dry the compartments well. This will prevent it from smelling bad and getting mold inside.
weekly.l5: Remove the protective cover to circulate air and take sun.

monthly.l1: Wax the hull and stainless steel hardware.
monthly.l2: Wash the anchor locker (bilge) and ropes, to avoid bad smell.
monthly.l3: Clean the refrigerator and always leave the door open to prevent mold.

six_months.l1: Clean the bathroom drains and siphons.
six_months.l4: Lubricate the canopy zipper with Silicone to prevent jamming.
six_months.l5: Apply Solid Vaseline on the battery terminal/post to prevent copper oxidation.
six_months.l8: Grease the propeller shaft with Blue Nautical Grease.

yearly.l1: Lubricate the trailer bearings and wheels. This will prevent corrosion and seizing.
yearly.l2: Update the nautical chart with the Port Authority.
yearly.l5: Check the validity of the flares (pyrotechnics). For NOMAD catamarans are used: 2 red star hand flares with parachute, 2 orange floating smoke signals, and 2 red hand flares.
yearly.l6: Check the sacrificial anodes located on the lower unit of the outboard motor.

hydrofoils.title: The Hydrofoil Revolution
hydrofoils.subtitle: First Tests on Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard Catamarans
hydrofoils.p1: Innovation and Technology in the Nautical World: In the current nautical scenario, innovation and technology are crucial elements to advance and improve vessel performance. Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard, known for its tradition and quality, took a significant step by initiating the first tests with the use of hydrofoils on its catamaran boats. This technology, already used in racing vessels and high-performance yachts, promises to revolutionize the leisure boat market, bringing considerable benefits in terms of speed, fuel efficiency, and stability.
hydrofoils.p2: What Are Hydrofoils? Hydrofoils are structures similar to submerged wings that, when installed on the hull of a vessel... When the boat reaches a certain speed, these wings create lift, raising the hull and significantly reducing resistance to displacement. This technology drastically reduces friction with water, increasing efficiency and allowing higher speeds with lower fuel consumption. In addition, the use of hydrofoils provides smoother navigation, reducing the impact of waves and offering greater comfort to occupants.
hydrofoils.p3:
First Tests and Results: The first tests carried out by Mastro D'Ascia Shipyard with its catamaran boats equipped with hydrofoils were extremely promising. The data collected reveal:
1. Efficiency Increase and Fuel Reduction up to 26%: The reduction of friction with water allowed a fuel saving of up to 26%, making the vessels more ecological and economical.
2. Top Speed Increase by 3 Knots: Boats equipped with hydrofoils reached a top speed 3 knots above conventional versions, standing out in terms of performance.
3. Greater Stability and Comfort: Navigation became more stable and comfortable, even in rough waters, providing a superior experience for occupants.
hydrofoils.p4: The Future of Catamaran Boats: The success of the first tests opens the way for a new era in Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard's catamaran boats. The introduction of hydrofoils not only improves the performance and efficiency of the vessels but also redefines the standard of comfort and sustainability in the nautical segment. With this innovation, the shipyard reaffirms its commitment to excellence and technological vanguard, consolidating its position always aligned with the newest technologies in the market.
hydrofoils.p5: Conclusion: The implementation of hydrofoils on catamaran boats represents a major evolution in the nautical environment. The first tests show a promising future, where speed, efficiency, and comfort walk side by side, raising the navigation experience to new levels. We are just at the beginning of a journey that promises to transform the way we live the sea, and Mastro D’Ascia Shipyard is at the forefront of this transformation.

safety.title: Safety Tips
safety.intro: Before going out to sail, make sure that all procedures and care regarding safety and the proper functioning of the boat have been taken. Check out some tips:

normam.content: Read and know the COLREGs (International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea) and the Maritime Authority Standards that establish the minimum safety requirements for vessels, especially NORMAM 01, NORMAM 02, NORMAM 03 and NORMAM 05.

periodic.content: Perform periodic maintenance of the vessel, without improvisation. Any carelessness could compromise the safety of those on board.

salvage.content: Perform a rigorous inspection of safety equipment and utensils. Pay special attention to life jackets, which must be in sufficient number for everyone who will embark. The jackets used on NOMAD catamarans are CLASS II.

fire.content: Inspect firefighting material, pay special attention to the expiration date and condition of the extinguishers. The indicated extinguishers are ABC powder.

inspect.content: Perform a hull survey, check the operation of bilge pumps, navigation lights, VHF radio equipment, and battery charge condition, as well as the engine crankcase oil level for 4-stroke engines and oil reservoir level in 2-stroke engines. Also check the integrity of the fuel level, and if there are no leaks in the engine compartment.

docs.content: Have documents on board proving your license, vessel registration, and mandatory insurance within the validity period.

charts.btn: Have nautical charts of the region
charts.content: Check if the nautical charts on board are compatible with the region where you intend to navigate, pay attention to lighthouses and nautical signaling.

docking.content: Check if the anchor has enough rope for safe anchoring. Remember that the indicated rope length for safe anchoring is five to seven times the depth of the place where you intend to anchor.

weather.content: Find out about the weather forecast released by the CHM and other agencies, and stay alert to the forecast of changes for the period. If everything is fine, plan your passage.

plan.content: Deliver the navigation plan or departure notice to the marina or club responsible for guarding the vessel; if not in a club or marina, let someone on land know where you plan to go with the vessel and what time you intend to return.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Mastro D’Ascia, accessible at https://www.mastrodascia.com.br, respects the privacy of its users and is committed to protecting personal data, in compliance with the General Data Protection Law (LGPD – Law No. 13.709/2018).
privacy.description: This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, and protect information when browsing our site.

s1.title: 1. Information Collection
s1.content: We may collect information voluntarily provided by the user, as well as data collected automatically during navigation, such as IP address, browser type, pages accessed, and time spent on the site.

s2.title: 2. Use of Cookies
s2.content: We use cookies to ensure the proper functioning of the site, improve your browsing experience, and analyze page performance and usage.
s2.note: Cookies are small files stored in your browser. You can manage or disable cookies in your browser settings at any time, aware that this may impact some site functionalities.

s3.title: 3. Google Analytics
s3.content: This site uses Google Analytics, an analysis service provided by Google LLC, which uses cookies to collect information anonymously about how users interact with the site.
s3.content2: The information generated by Google Analytics cookies includes data such as pages visited, time spent, and origin of access, being used exclusively for statistical analysis and improvement of the user experience.
s3.content3: These data may be stored on servers located outside Brazil, in accordance with Google's privacy practices.

s4.title: 4. Purpose of Data Use
s4.content:
• Ensure site functioning
• Improve browsing experience
• Analyze access and performance metrics
• Comply with legal and regulatory obligations

s5.title: 5. Data Sharing
s5.content: Mastro D’Ascia does not sell, rent, or share personal data with third parties, except when necessary for site operation, compliance with legal obligations, or by court order.

s6.title: 6. Storage and Security
s6.content: We adopt appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, loss, alteration, or improper disclosure.
s6.content2: Data is stored only for the time necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this policy, unless required by law.

s7.title: 7. Data Subject Rights
s7.content:
• Access to your personal data
• Correction of incomplete or outdated data
• Deletion of data, when applicable
• Revocation of consent

s8.title: 8. Links to Third-Party Sites
s8.content: Our site may contain links to external sites. Mastro D’Ascia is not responsible for the privacy practices or content of these sites.

s9.title: 9. Changes to this Policy
s9.content: This Privacy Policy may be updated periodically. We recommend that the user review this content regularly.

s10.title: 10. Contact
s10.content: In case of doubts about this Privacy Policy or the processing of personal data, contact us through the channels available on the site mastrodascia.com.br.

description.p1: Explore the waters with confidence aboard the NOMAD 7CC catamaran, the perfect companion for your journeys at sea.
description.p2: As the lightest and most compact member of the Nomad family, its smooth and stable navigation provides an incomparable sense of safety, even at high speeds.
description.p3: Unanimously elected as the best cost-benefit option among the brand's models, the NOMAD 7CC is the ideal choice for fishermen who value economy. Request a quote and get ready to explore the seas with comfort and efficiency.

7xf.title: Nomad 7 XF
7xf.magazine_title: Check out what they said about the 7 XF!

description.p1: The Nomad 7XF stands out for offering comfort items usually found in larger boats. Standard items include anti-mold courvin upholstery, hydraulic steering, electric toilet, and 316L stainless steel guardrail. In addition, it has a capacity for 8 passengers and overnight stay for 2 people, making it ideal for both day and night trips.
description.p2: Options range from an electric refrigerator and electric winch to a bow solarium and underwater lights, allowing customizations that further raise the level of comfort on board. The result is a catamaran that offers a comfortable navigation experience, suitable for those seeking the best performance combined with luxury.
description.p3: These features make the Nomad 7XF an exceptional choice for those who want a versatile, compact, and efficient boat.

description.p1: The Nomad 7XS is the smallest model from the Mastro D'Ascia shipyard, developed with unique specifications to meet NORMAN 12 requirements as a pilot support boat. With compact dimensions, the Nomad 7XS offers the maneuverability and agility necessary for support operations and navigation in varied conditions.
description.p2: Built with the same quality and attention to detail as the larger models, the Nomad 7XS uses advanced construction techniques, such as vacuum infusion, ensuring a strong and lightweight hull, without the use of wood, which increases durability and facilitates maintenance. This model is also equipped with several standard items that ensure comfort and safety, such as anti-mold upholstery, hydraulic steering, and customization options that allow adapting it to the specific needs of the operation.
description.p3: In addition to its robustness, the Nomad 7XS was designed to be economical and efficient, essential characteristics for a pilot support boat, which demands continuous reliability and performance. This model is the ideal solution for support operations where space and maneuverability are crucial, maintaining the quality and performance that are trademarks of the Mastro D'Ascia shipyard.

description.p1: The Nomad 8CC is a catamaran model developed to be a high-performance solution, especially in sport fishing activities that demand agility and efficiency.
description.p2: This model is built using vacuum infusion technology, which not only reduces the vessel's weight but also increases its strength and durability. The Nomad 8CC hull is optimized to provide fast planing and smooth navigation, even in adverse conditions, characteristics that are crucial for those who need to cover large distances at sea.
description.p3: The Nomad 8CC combines all these features with a robust and functional design, making it an ideal choice for fishermen who require a reliable, economical, and high-performance vessel, capable of facing the challenges of the sea with agility and low operating cost.

description.p1: With a spacious and well-equipped cabin, this model offers the maximum in convenience and safety for your adventures at sea. Enjoy an unparalleled navigation experience, protected from the elements while exploring new horizons.
description.p2: The NOMAD 8XF has several standard items that enhance navigation, ensuring exceptional performance and a smooth journey. From advanced navigation systems to comfort features, every detail has been carefully designed to provide an unmatched experience at sea.
description.p3: In addition to its high performance, the NOMAD 8XF also stands out for its incomparable style, with elegant lines and a modern design. Get ready to sail with style and leave your mark on every trip aboard the NOMAD 8XF.

description.p1: The NOMAD 8 XS is a pilot or service boat. Designed to be versatile and suitable for any training and learning of maneuvers and driving.
description.p2: Equipped with a variety of safety items and modern equipment, it offers a safe and reliable platform for beginners to improve their navigation skills.
description.p3: Its excellent seaworthiness makes training lessons more effective, while its robust construction ensures durability and confidence in all practice sessions and services at sea.

description.p1: The NOMAD 8.5CC catamaran is a direct response to the demands of the most demanding fishermen. With a range of carefully integrated features and solutions, this model is designed to adapt to a variety of fishing styles and techniques, ensuring a personalized and efficient experience.
description.p2: Its lightness does not compromise its robustness, the result of a meticulous structural design and the use of the best materials available, ensuring reliable navigation in all conditions.
description.p3: Capable of reaching planing almost instantly, it provides smooth and stable navigation, even in rough seas. This efficiency not only reduces fuel consumption but also increases its range, allowing for longer and safer journeys.

description.p1: The NOMAD 8.5 XF is the definitive choice for those seeking versatility and comfort in a single boat. With its spacious cabin and a large protected cockpit area, this model offers the perfect balance between functionality and convenience, making it ideal for both fishing and family outings.
description.p2: In addition to its exceptional adaptability, the NOMAD 8.5 XF offers impressive performance. Whether exploring new fishing spots or crossing the waters on a relaxing ride, the NOMAD 8.5 XF will deliver a smooth and efficient journey.
description.p3: With its unique combination of space, protection, performance, and versatility, this catamaran redefines the concept of seaworthiness, ensuring memorable experiences for you at sea.

description.p1: The Nomad 8.5 XS is ready to face any challenge and deliver exceptional performance. It is a versatile and adaptable catamaran for a variety of aquatic practices and services.
description.p2: With an intelligent and functional design, this model offers the space, safety, and flexibility needed to meet the demands of various activities and services.
description.p3: Equipped with specialized features and a robust structure, the Nomad 8.5 XS is ideal for practical navigation training, providing an effective and safe learning experience. In addition, its responsiveness and maneuverability make it a reliable choice for various services at sea.

description.p1: The definitive fusion for those seeking adventure and comfort, this boat was meticulously designed for fishing and leisure enthusiasts.
description.p2: Its robust and elegant design offers an incomparable navigation experience, while the spacious cabin provides the perfect refuge to escape adverse conditions or relax after an exciting day at sea.
description.p3: With a variety of customization options available, you can adapt the Titan Cabin to your specific needs, whether you are an avid fisherman or someone looking for quiet moments with friends and family on the ocean.

videos.sky_sea: Where the sky meets the sea

description.p1: With its spacious cabin and central control, this catamaran redefines the meaning of sailing with style and delivers performance in a luxurious and comfortable environment for the most demanding sailors.
description.p2: Every detail of the Titan Commuter has been carefully crafted to provide a smooth and exciting journey. Its imposing size and modern design ensure protection against any adversity, providing an unparalleled sense of safety while you navigate the waters.
description.p3: Combining strength, comfort, and performance, this catamaran is the right choice to brave the seas with confidence towards new discoveries.

videos.sky_sea: Where the sky meets the sea

description.p1: Equipped with a center console offering more comfort and practicality, this catamaran delivers everything expected from a high-quality fishing boat.
description.p2: The Titan CC features state-of-the-art equipment and ample fishing space, providing a complete and satisfying experience for the most demanding sailors. Its quality and safety are unmatched, ensuring tranquility and autonomy to sail far from the coast.
description.p3: Prepare yourself for a unique and exciting adventure, where every moment is marked by the comfort, quality, and safety that only the Titan CC can offer.
